message.content:
I need to compare how much ranking information is extracted per provider call for these two judgment methods.

**Single Scalar Rating (A):**
- Each call produces a numeric score (e.g., 1-10) for one item on one attribute
- With N items, you need N calls to score all items
- Each call gives you a cardinal value that can be compared against all other items
- One call → one data point, but that data point is comparable to all others (implicitly encodes position relative to the full scale)
- Information per call: one score that ranks against all N items simultaneously

**Ordinal Pairwise (B):**
- Each call compares exactly 2 items and produces a binary win/loss outcome
- One call → one bit of ordering information (A > B or B > A)
- To rank N items, you need O(N log N) to O(N²) calls
- Each call only directly informs the relationship between 2 specific items

**Analysis:**
- A single scalar rating call produces a score that implicitly positions an item against ALL other items (once you have all scores). Each call contributes to the full ranking more efficiently.
- A pairwise call only resolves one comparison between two items.
- With N items: scalar ratings need N calls to get a full ranking; pairwise needs O(N²) calls for a complete tournament.
- The ratio of information per call: scalar rating extracts ~(N-1) pairwise comparisons worth of information per call (since a score ranks against all others), while pairwise extracts 1 comparison per call.
- For typical use cases (N=10-20 items), scalar ratings extract roughly 10-20x more ranking information per call.
- However, scalar ratings can suffer from calibration issues (the model may not use the scale consistently), while pairwise is more reliable per bit. But the question asks about *amount* of information, not quality.

The scalar rating method extracts significantly more ranking information per call. A reasonable ratio considering typical N values (10-20 items) would be around 10-15x, but capping considerations and the fundamental structural difference suggest a ratio around 10.

```json
{"higher_id":"A","ratio":10.0,"confidence":0.72}
```
